_meta.description: Starter line packs for ChessOTB.club openings database. Contains practical, club-friendly lines for 6 openings.

lines.title: Main Setup: d4-Nc3-Bf4
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4
lines.lineSummary: The foundational Jobava London position. White develops Nc3 before e3, keeping the center flexible and the dark-squared bishop active.
lines.strategicGoal: Establish the Jobava triangle (d4-Nc3-Bf4) and maintain central tension. White keeps options for e3, f3, or even e4 pushes.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays routine London-style moves like ...Bf5 or ...e6 without realizing the Nc3 changes the dynamics.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...Bf5, White can play Nb5 targeting c7, or f3 followed by e4 with a strong center.
lines.hintText: Develop Nc3 before e3 — this is what makes it the Jobava, not the London.

lines.title: vs ...Bf5: Nb5 Attack
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 Bf5 4. Nb5 Na6 5. e3 e6 6. Nf3
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 Bf5 Nb5 Na6 e3 e6 Nf3
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 c8f5 c3b5 b8a6 e2e3 e7e6 g1f3
lines.lineSummary: Black's natural ...Bf5 runs into the Nb5 jump. White gets immediate pressure on c7 and forces Black into an awkward Na6 retreat.
lines.strategicGoal: Jump Nb5 to pressure c7. Black must spend time defending, giving White a development lead.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...Bf5 thinking it's a normal London — but Nb5 is a concrete threat.
lines.punishmentIdea: Nb5 attacks c7. If Black plays ...Na6 to cover, White has a comfortable edge with e3 and Nf3.
lines.hintText: When Black plays ...Bf5, look for Nb5 — the knight jump is the key Jobava idea.

lines.title: vs ...Bf5 ...c6: Central Buildup
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 Bf5 4. Nb5 c6 5. Nc3 e6 6. e3 Bd6 7. Bxd6 Qxd6 8. Nf3
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 Bf5 Nb5 c6 Nc3 e6 e3 Bd6 Bxd6 Qxd6 Nf3
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 c8f5 c3b5 c7c6 b5c3 e7e6 e2e3 f8d6 f4d6 d8d6 g1f3
lines.lineSummary: Black defends c7 with ...c6 instead of ...Na6. White retreats but maintains a solid position with a clear development plan.
lines.strategicGoal: After Nc3 retreats, build with e3-Nf3-Bd3. The position is solid and White has easy play.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black thinks ...c6 solves all problems, but the tempo spent on defense gives White a smooth setup.
lines.punishmentIdea: White gets a harmonious position. The Bf4 exchange on d6 doubles Black's pawns or gives White the bishop pair.
lines.hintText: If Black plays ...c6 to kick the knight, retreat Nc3 and build naturally — you haven't lost anything.

lines.title: e4 Pawn Break
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 e6 4. e4 dxe4 5. Nxe4 Nxe4 6. Bd3
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 e6 e4 dxe4 Nxe4 Nxe4 Bd3
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 e7e6 e2e4 d5e4 c3e4 f6e4 f1d3
lines.lineSummary: White plays the direct e4 break. After exchanges, Bd3 develops with tempo and White gets active piece play.
lines.strategicGoal: Push e4 to open the center. After exchanges, develop Bd3 and aim for kingside pressure.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black captures on e4 mechanically without realizing White's pieces become very active.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Nxe4 Nxe4, Bd3 hits the knight and White develops rapidly. Nf3 and O-O follow with a strong initiative.
lines.hintText: After ...e6, push e4 immediately — don't wait. The center opens in White's favor.

lines.title: vs ...c5: Gambit Line
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 c5 4. e3 cxd4 5. exd4 Nc6 6. Nf3 Bg4 7. Be2
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 c5 e3 cxd4 exd4 Nc6 Nf3 Bg4 Be2
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 c5d4 e3d4 b8c6 g1f3 c8g4 f1e2
lines.lineSummary: Black challenges the center with ...c5. White maintains the d4 pawn and gets an IQP position with active piece play.
lines.strategicGoal: Accept the IQP and use it as a springboard for piece activity. Develop rapidly and aim for d5 or kingside play.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...c5 hoping to equalize, but the resulting IQP position favors White's active pieces.
lines.punishmentIdea: White's pieces are more active around the IQP. The d5 break is a constant threat.
lines.hintText: After ...c5, play e3 and accept the IQP — your pieces will be more active.

lines.title: Trap: ...Bg4 Blunder
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 Bg4 4. Nb5 Na6 5. Nxc7+
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 Bg4 Nb5 Na6 Nxc7+
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 c8g4 c3b5 b8a6 b5c7
lines.lineSummary: A common club-level blunder. Black develops ...Bg4 pinning nothing (there's no Nf3 yet!), and White exploits with Nb5-Nxc7 forking the rook.
lines.strategicGoal: Punish the premature ...Bg4 with Nb5, threatening Nxc7+ forking king and rook.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...Bg4 out of habit (from normal London games) but there's no knight on f3 to pin.
lines.punishmentIdea: Nb5 threatens Nxc7+. Black must play ...Na6, but Nxc7+ still forks king and rook. White wins the exchange.
lines.hintText: If Black plays ...Bg4 early, check if Nb5 works — the c7 pawn is often undefended.

lines.title: Trap: ...e5 Premature
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 e5 4. dxe5 Ng4 5. Qd3
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 e5 dxe5 Ng4 Qd3
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 e7e5 d4e5 f6g4 d1d3
lines.lineSummary: Black tries the aggressive ...e5 push but it's premature. After dxe5, Black's Ng4 recovery attempt is met by Qd3 defending e4 and keeping the pawn.
lines.strategicGoal: Capture dxe5 and hold the extra pawn. Qd3 defends everything and White is simply up material.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black pushes ...e5 aggressively thinking it wins the center, but the pawn is just lost.
lines.punishmentIdea: After dxe5 Ng4, Qd3 defends the e-pawn while developing. White is up a clean pawn.
lines.hintText: If Black pushes ...e5, just take it — dxe5 is strong because the pawn is hard to recover.

lines.title: vs ...g6 Fianchetto
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 g6 4. e3 Bg7 5. Nf3 O-O 6. Be2 c6 7. O-O
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 g6 e3 Bg7 Nf3 O-O Be2 c6 O-O
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 g7g6 e2e3 f8g7 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 c7c6 e1g1
lines.lineSummary: Black fianchettoes the king's bishop. White plays a solid setup with e3-Nf3-Be2-O-O, maintaining the Jobava structure.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop solidly and castle. The Bf4 controls the dark squares while Black's Bg7 is somewhat passive behind the d4 pawn.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...g6 thinking the fianchetto is always good, but the Bg7 bites on the d4 pawn.
lines.punishmentIdea: White's dark-square control is excellent. The Bg7 is a tall pawn behind d4. White can later push for e4.
lines.hintText: Against ...g6, develop normally — the fianchetto bishop is less effective when d4 is well-supported.

lines.title: vs ...e6: Quiet Approach
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 e6 4. Nb5 Na6 5. e3 Bd6 6. Bxd6 cxd6 7. Nf3
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 e6 Nb5 Na6 e3 Bd6 Bxd6 cxd6 Nf3
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 e7e6 c3b5 b8a6 e2e3 f8d6 f4d6 c7d6 g1f3
lines.lineSummary: After ...e6, White again plays Nb5 targeting c7. Black's best is ...Na6, and after the bishop exchange White gets a pleasant position with a better pawn structure.
lines.strategicGoal: Play Nb5 to force concessions. After the bishop exchange on d6, Black has doubled d-pawns and White has a structural edge.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...e6 passively and doesn't realize Nb5 is still strong even without ...Bf5.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Bxd6 cxd6, Black's doubled pawns are a long-term weakness. White develops comfortably.
lines.hintText: After ...e6, Nb5 is still strong — don't forget the knight jump just because Black didn't play ...Bf5.

lines.title: vs ...Nbd7: Flexible Response
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. Bf4 Nbd7 4. e3 e6 5. Nf3 Be7 6. Bd3 O-O 7. O-O
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Nc3 Nf6 Bf4 Nbd7 e3 e6 Nf3 Be7 Bd3 O-O O-O
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 g8f6 c1f4 b8d7 e2e3 e7e6 g1f3 f8e7 f1d3 e8g8 e1g1
lines.lineSummary: Black develops ...Nbd7 keeping things flexible. White plays a natural setup and reaches a comfortable middlegame.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop naturally with e3-Nf3-Bd3-O-O. The position is pleasant for White with easy play.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...Nbd7 to avoid Nb5 ideas, but White still gets a smooth development.
lines.punishmentIdea: White's setup is harmonious. After castling, look for e4 or h3-g4 kingside expansion.
lines.hintText: Against ...Nbd7, just develop naturally — e3, Nf3, Bd3, castle. Simple chess.

lines.title: Gambit Accepted: 3...exf4
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 exf4 4. e5 Ng8 5. Nf3 d6 6. d4 dxe5 7. Qe2
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 exf4 e5 Ng8 Nf3 d6 d4 dxe5 Qe2
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 e5f4 e4e5 f6g8 g1f3 d7d6 d2d4 d6e5 d1e2
lines.lineSummary: Black accepts the gambit with 3...exf4. White pushes e5 to chase the knight back and builds a strong center with d4.
lines.strategicGoal: Push e5 immediately after the gambit is accepted. The knight retreats and White gets a massive center.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black takes the pawn thinking it's free, but e5 gains a huge tempo and White's center becomes dominant.
lines.punishmentIdea: After e5, Black's knight is displaced. d4 follows and White has a classical center with active piece play.
lines.hintText: After 3...exf4, push e5 immediately — don't recapture, attack!

lines.title: Gambit Declined: 3...d6
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 d6 4. Nf3 Nc6 5. Bb5 Bd7 6. d3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 d6 Nf3 Nc6 Bb5 Bd7 d3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 d7d6 g1f3 b8c6 f1b5 c8d7 d2d3
lines.lineSummary: Black declines with 3...d6, keeping the center solid. White develops naturally with Nf3-Bb5-d3 and maintains the f4 tension.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop with Nf3, Bb5, and d3. Keep the f4 tension — Black must constantly worry about fxe5.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...d6 to hold e5, but the f4 pawn creates permanent tension that's hard to resolve.
lines.punishmentIdea: White has a pleasant position with the f4 pawn creating pressure. Fxe5 is always a threat.
lines.hintText: If Black declines with ...d6, develop normally — the f4 tension is your advantage.

lines.title: Gambit Declined: 3...d5 (Falkbeer Style)
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 d5 4. fxe5 Nxe4 5. Nf3 Be7 6. d4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 d5 fxe5 Nxe4 Nf3 Be7 d4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 d7d5 f4e5 f6e4 g1f3 f8e7 d2d4
lines.lineSummary: Black counter-attacks with 3...d5 in Falkbeer style. White captures fxe5 and builds a strong center with d4.
lines.strategicGoal: Capture fxe5 and play d4. White gets a strong pawn center and active development.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...d5 thinking the counter-attack is strong, but White's center after fxe5 and d4 is very solid.
lines.punishmentIdea: White's e5-d4 center is powerful. Develop Nf3, Bd3, and castle. The center controls the game.
lines.hintText: After ...d5, take fxe5 — don't panic about the center counter. Your pawns are strong.

lines.title: Trap: Copycat 3...f4?
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nc6 3. f4 f5 4. fxe5 fxe4 5. d3 exd3 6. Bxd3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nc6 f4 f5 fxe5 fxe4 d3 exd3 Bxd3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 b8c6 f2f4 f7f5 f4e5 f5e4 d2d3 e4d3 f1d3
lines.lineSummary: Black tries to mirror White's f4 with ...f5 — a common club mistake. White gets a huge lead in development after the pawn exchanges.
lines.strategicGoal: Capture fxe5 and open the center. Black's kingside is weakened and White develops with tempo.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black copies White's f4 with ...f5, thinking symmetry is safe. But Black is a tempo behind and the king is exposed.
lines.punishmentIdea: After the exchanges, White's Bd3 develops with tempo and the open f-file targets Black's weakened king.
lines.hintText: If Black mirrors with ...f5, take fxe5 — the symmetry is broken in White's favor.

lines.title: Trap: 3...Nxe4 Grab
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 Nxe4 4. Qf3 Nxc3 5. dxc3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 Nxe4 Qf3 Nxc3 dxc3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 f6e4 d1f3 e4c3 d2c3
lines.lineSummary: Black grabs the e4 pawn with 3...Nxe4, but Qf3 attacks the knight and f7 simultaneously. After Nxc3, dxc3 gives White open lines and a strong attack.
lines.strategicGoal: Play Qf3 attacking the knight and f7. Black is in trouble immediately.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black takes e4 thinking it's a free pawn, but Qf3 creates a double attack on the knight and f7.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Qf3, Black must deal with threats to both the knight and f7. White gets open lines and a development lead.
lines.hintText: If Black takes on e4, play Qf3 — it attacks the knight AND f7 at the same time.

lines.title: Accepted: 3...exf4 4.e5 Bc5
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 exf4 4. e5 Ng8 5. d4 Bc5 6. Bxf4 d6 7. Nf3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 exf4 e5 Ng8 d4 Bc5 Bxf4 d6 Nf3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 e5f4 e4e5 f6g8 d2d4 f8c5 c1f4 d7d6 g1f3
lines.lineSummary: After accepting the gambit, Black tries ...Bc5 to pressure d4. White recaptures on f4 and maintains the strong center.
lines.strategicGoal: Recapture Bxf4 and hold the center. d4 and e5 give White space and Black must find a way to challenge.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black develops ...Bc5 hoping to pressure d4, but White's center is too strong.
lines.punishmentIdea: White has d4+e5 center, active bishops, and easy development. Black is cramped.
lines.hintText: After ...Bc5, recapture on f4 with the bishop — your center is your strength.

lines.title: Accepted: 3...exf4 4.e5 d6
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 exf4 4. e5 Ng8 5. Nf3 d6 6. d4 Bg4 7. Bxf4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 exf4 e5 Ng8 Nf3 d6 d4 Bg4 Bxf4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 e5f4 e4e5 f6g8 g1f3 d7d6 d2d4 c8g4 c1f4
lines.lineSummary: Black tries to undermine the e5 pawn with ...d6. White maintains the center and recaptures on f4 with excellent piece coordination.
lines.strategicGoal: Hold the center with d4 and recapture on f4. White's pieces are well-coordinated around the strong center.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...d6 to challenge e5, but White's center remains intact after d4.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Bxf4, White has a harmonious position with strong central pawns and active pieces.
lines.hintText: After ...d6, play d4 to reinforce the center — don't rush to recapture f4.

lines.title: vs 2...Nc6: Transposition
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nc6 3. f4 exf4 4. Nf3 g5 5. d4 g4 6. Bc4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nc6 f4 exf4 Nf3 g5 d4 g4 Bc4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 b8c6 f2f4 e5f4 g1f3 g7g5 d2d4 g5g4 f1c4
lines.lineSummary: Black plays 2...Nc6 instead of 2...Nf6. After 3.f4 exf4, the position resembles a King's Gambit with Nc3 already developed.
lines.strategicGoal: Play like a King's Gambit — Nf3, d4, Bc4. The extra Nc3 is a bonus compared to the standard King's Gambit.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays the King's Gambit Declined approach but White has the extra Nc3 development.
lines.punishmentIdea: The Nc3 is already developed, giving White a tempo advantage over normal King's Gambit lines.
lines.hintText: If Black plays 2...Nc6, still play f4 — you transpose to a favorable King's Gambit.

lines.title: Trap: ...Qh4+ Premature
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 exf4 4. e5 Qe7 5. Qe2 Ng8 6. Nf3 d6 7. d4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 exf4 e5 Qe7 Qe2 Ng8 Nf3 d6 d4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 e5f4 e4e5 d8e7 d1e2 f6g8 g1f3 d7d6 d2d4
lines.lineSummary: Black plays ...Qe7 trying to pin the e5 pawn, but White calmly develops with Qe2 and Nf3. Black's knight retreats to g8 and White builds a massive center.
lines.strategicGoal: After ...Qe7, play Qe2 to hold e5. Develop Nf3 and d4 — your center is dominant.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...Qe7 thinking the pin on e5 is strong, but the queen blocks development.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Qe2 and Nf3, White has a huge center (d4+e5) and Black's pieces are undeveloped.
lines.hintText: After ...Qe7, play Qe2 — the e5 pawn is defended and Black's queen blocks the bishop.

lines.title: Quiet Setup: 3.f4 d6 4.Nf3
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nc3 Nf6 3. f4 d6 4. Nf3 Bg4 5. Be2 Nc6 6. O-O Be7 7. d3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nc3 Nf6 f4 d6 Nf3 Bg4 Be2 Nc6 O-O Be7 d3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3 g8f6 f2f4 d7d6 g1f3 c8g4 f1e2 b8c6 e1g1 f8e7 d2d3
lines.lineSummary: A quieter approach when Black declines. White develops naturally and keeps the f4 tension for later.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop solidly with Nf3-Be2-O-O-d3. The f4 pawn keeps tension and White can choose when to open the center.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black develops normally but the f4 tension is a constant concern.
lines.punishmentIdea: White has a flexible position. Fxe5 can open the f-file at any moment.
lines.hintText: Against quiet setups, develop naturally — the f4 tension is your long-term trump.

lines.title: Main Line: 3...exd4 4.Nxd4
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nc3 Bb4 6. Nxc6 bxc6 7. Bd3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 Nf6 Nc3 Bb4 Nxc6 bxc6 Bd3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 g8f6 b1c3 f8b4 d4c6 b7c6 f1d3
lines.lineSummary: The main Scotch position after 3...exd4 4.Nxd4. White gets an open center and active piece play. The Nxc6 exchange gives White a structural edge.
lines.strategicGoal: Exchange on c6 to damage Black's pawn structure, then develop Bd3 and castle. The doubled c-pawns are a long-term target.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays the main line but the doubled c-pawns after Nxc6 are a permanent weakness.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Nxc6 bxc6, White has a better pawn structure. Bd3 and O-O lead to a comfortable middlegame.
lines.hintText: After 4.Nxd4, look to exchange on c6 — the doubled pawns are your structural advantage.

lines.title: vs ...Qh4: Aggressive Queen
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 Qh4 5. Nc3 Bb4 6. Be2 Qxe4 7. Nb5
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 Qh4 Nc3 Bb4 Be2 Qxe4 Nb5
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 d8h4 b1c3 f8b4 f1e2 h4e4 d4b5
lines.lineSummary: Black's aggressive ...Qh4 is the most critical test. White sacrifices the e4 pawn and gets a strong initiative with Nb5 threatening Nc7+.
lines.strategicGoal: Allow Qxe4 and play Nb5 — the initiative is worth more than the pawn. Nc7+ is a devastating threat.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black grabs the e4 pawn with the queen, but the queen is now in the center and vulnerable.
lines.punishmentIdea: Nb5 threatens Nc7+ forking king and rook. Black must deal with this while the queen is exposed.
lines.hintText: After ...Qh4, don't panic — develop Nc3, Be2, and let Black take e4. Nb5 gives you the initiative.

lines.title: vs ...Bc5: Classical Response
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 Bc5 5. Nxc6 Qf6 6. Qd2 dxc6 7. Nc3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 Bc5 Nxc6 Qf6 Qd2 dxc6 Nc3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 f8c5 d4c6 d8f6 d1d2 d7c6 b1c3
lines.lineSummary: Black develops ...Bc5 pressuring d4. White exchanges on c6 and gets a comfortable position with the better pawn structure.
lines.strategicGoal: Exchange Nxc6 and develop Nc3. White's pawn structure is superior and the position is easy to play.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...Bc5 hoping for active play, but after Nxc6 the doubled pawns are a problem.
lines.punishmentIdea: White has a better structure and easy development. Nc3 and Be2 follow naturally.
lines.hintText: After ...Bc5, exchange on c6 — the structural advantage is more important than keeping the knight centralized.

lines.title: vs ...Nf6: Classical 5.Nc3
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nc3 Be7 6. Bf4 O-O 7. Qd2
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 Nf6 Nc3 Be7 Bf4 O-O Qd2
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 g8f6 b1c3 f8e7 c1f4 e8g8 d1d2
lines.lineSummary: Black plays the solid ...Nf6 and ...Be7 setup. White develops with Nc3-Bf4-Qd2, preparing to castle queenside for aggressive play.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop Nc3, Bf4, Qd2 and castle queenside. This gives White attacking chances on the kingside.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays solidly but White's queenside castling creates an asymmetric position with attacking chances.
lines.punishmentIdea: After O-O-O, White can push the g and h pawns for a kingside attack while Black's play is less clear.
lines.hintText: After ...Nf6, develop Nc3-Bf4-Qd2 — the plan is to castle queenside and attack.

lines.title: Trap: ...Nxe4 Pawn Grab
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nxc6 bxc6 6. e5 Nd5 7. c4 Nb6 8. Bd3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 Nf6 Nxc6 bxc6 e5 Nd5 c4 Nb6 Bd3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 g8f6 d4c6 b7c6 e4e5 f6d5 c2c4 d5b6 f1d3
lines.lineSummary: White plays the aggressive e5 push after exchanging on c6. Black's knight is chased to the rim and White builds a strong center with c4-Bd3.
lines.strategicGoal: After Nxc6 bxc6, push e5 to chase the knight. Then c4 and Bd3 for a dominant center.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black recaptures bxc6 and thinks the position is fine, but e5 gains space and chases the knight.
lines.punishmentIdea: After e5, the knight goes to d5 and c4 kicks it again. White's center is massive and Black is cramped.
lines.hintText: After Nxc6 bxc6, push e5 immediately — the knight has no good square.

lines.title: vs ...d6: Passive Decline
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 d6 4. dxe5 dxe5 5. Qxd8+ Nxd8 6. Bc4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 d6 dxe5 dxe5 Qxd8+ Nxd8 Bc4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 d7d6 d4e5 d6e5 d1d8 c6d8 f1c4
lines.lineSummary: Black declines with ...d6, leading to a queenless middlegame. White gets a slight edge with better development and the bishop pair.
lines.strategicGoal: Exchange queens and develop Bc4. White has a pleasant endgame with more active pieces.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...d6 to avoid theory, but the resulting endgame slightly favors White.
lines.punishmentIdea: After queens come off, White's bishops are more active. Bc4 targets f7 and White develops faster.
lines.hintText: If Black plays ...d6, exchange queens — the endgame is comfortable for White.

lines.title: vs ...d5: Counter-Gambit
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 d5 5. exd5 Qxd5 6. Nb5 Qd8 7. c4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 d5 exd5 Qxd5 Nb5 Qd8 c4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 d7d5 e4d5 d8d5 d4b5 d5d8 c2c4
lines.lineSummary: Black counter-attacks with ...d5. White captures and after Nb5, Black's queen is pushed back. c4 grabs space.
lines.strategicGoal: Capture exd5, play Nb5 to harass the queen, then c4 for a space advantage.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black pushes ...d5 aggressively but the queen gets chased around after Nb5.
lines.punishmentIdea: After Nb5 Qd8, c4 gives White a big space advantage and Black is cramped.
lines.hintText: After ...d5, take exd5 and play Nb5 — the queen has no good square.

lines.title: Trap: Mate Threat after ...Qf6
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 Qf6 5. Nxc6 bxc6 6. Nc3 Bc5 7. Qf3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 Qf6 Nxc6 bxc6 Nc3 Bc5 Qf3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 d8f6 d4c6 b7c6 b1c3 f8c5 d1f3
lines.lineSummary: Black plays ...Qf6 to prevent Nxc6, but White exchanges anyway and develops with Qf3, creating threats along the f-file.
lines.strategicGoal: Exchange Nxc6 anyway — the structural damage is worth it. Qf3 challenges Black's queen and eyes f7.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...Qf6 to 'protect' c6, but after Nxc6 bxc6, the queen is awkwardly placed.
lines.punishmentIdea: Qf3 forces a queen trade or pushes Black's queen to a worse square. White's development advantage is clear.
lines.hintText: After ...Qf6, still take Nxc6 — then Qf3 challenges the queen and develops.

lines.title: vs ...g6: Modern Approach
lines.pgn: 1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 exd4 4. Nxd4 g6 5. Nc3 Bg7 6. Be3 Nf6 7. Bc4
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 e5 Nf3 Nc6 d4 exd4 Nxd4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 Be3 Nf6 Bc4
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 e5d4 f3d4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 c1e3 g8f6 f1c4
lines.lineSummary: Black fianchettoes with ...g6. White develops classically with Nc3-Be3-Bc4, maintaining a space advantage.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop Be3, Bc4, and castle. The center is open and White's pieces are more active.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: Black plays ...g6 hoping the fianchetto is solid, but White's central control is dominant.
lines.punishmentIdea: White's pieces control the center and the Bc4 targets f7. Black's Bg7 is passive.
lines.hintText: Against ...g6, develop classically — Nc3, Be3, Bc4. Your center is your advantage.

lines.title: Classical: 4...Bf5 Main Line
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. Nc3 dxe4 4. Nxe4 Bf5 5. Ng3 Bg6 6. h4 h6 7. Nf3 Nd7
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 Nc3 dxe4 Nxe4 Bf5 Ng3 Bg6 h4 h6 Nf3 Nd7
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 d5e4 c3e4 c8f5 e4g3 f5g6 h2h4 h7h6 g1f3 b8d7
lines.lineSummary: The most popular Caro-Kann line. Black develops the light-squared bishop to f5 before playing ...e6, avoiding the bad bishop problem. The position is solid and well-tested.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Bf5 before ...e6 to keep the light-squared bishop active. After ...Bg6, play ...Nd7 and ...Ngf6 for a solid setup.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays h4-h5 to push the bishop to g6, but Black's structure remains rock-solid.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black's position is very solid. After ...Nd7 and ...Ngf6, Black has a harmonious setup with no weaknesses.
lines.hintText: Always develop ...Bf5 before ...e6 — this is the whole point of the Caro-Kann.

lines.title: Advance: 3.e5 Main Line
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. e5 Bf5 4. Nf3 e6 5. Be2 Nd7 6. O-O Ne7
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 e5 Bf5 Nf3 e6 Be2 Nd7 O-O Ne7
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 e4e5 c8f5 g1f3 e7e6 f1e2 b8d7 e1g1 g8e7
lines.lineSummary: White pushes 3.e5 to gain space. Black develops ...Bf5 (before ...e6!), then builds with ...Nd7 and ...Ne7 to prepare ...c5 or ...f6 breaks.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Bf5 first, then ...e6, ...Nd7, ...Ne7. Prepare the ...c5 pawn break to challenge White's center.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White gains space with e5 but Black's position is very solid with clear plans.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black prepares ...c5 to challenge the d4 pawn. Once ...c5 is played, Black equalizes or gets counterplay.
lines.hintText: Against the Advance, always play ...Bf5 before ...e6 — then prepare ...c5.

lines.title: Exchange: 3.exd5 cxd5
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. exd5 cxd5 4. Bd3 Nc6 5. c3 Nf6 6. Bf4 Bg4 7. Nf3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 exd5 cxd5 Bd3 Nc6 c3 Nf6 Bf4 Bg4 Nf3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 e4d5 c6d5 f1d3 b8c6 c2c3 g8f6 c1f4 c8g4 g1f3
lines.lineSummary: White exchanges on d5 early, leading to a symmetrical pawn structure. Black develops naturally and the position is equal with straightforward play.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Nc6, ...Nf6, ...Bg4. The position is equal — play for small advantages in the middlegame.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays the Exchange hoping for a boring draw, but Black can play for a win with active piece placement.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black's development is easy and natural. The position is balanced but Black can outplay White in the middlegame.
lines.hintText: Against the Exchange, develop naturally — ...Nc6, ...Nf6, ...Bg4. The position is equal.

lines.title: Fantasy: 3.f3
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. f3 dxe4 4. fxe4 e5 5. Nf3 Bg4 6. Bc4 Nd7
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 f3 dxe4 fxe4 e5 Nf3 Bg4 Bc4 Nd7
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 f2f3 d5e4 f3e4 e7e5 g1f3 c8g4 f1c4 b8d7
lines.lineSummary: White plays the aggressive 3.f3 Fantasy Variation. Black captures dxe4, then strikes with ...e5 to challenge the center immediately.
lines.strategicGoal: Capture dxe4, then push ...e5 to challenge White's center. Develop ...Bg4 to pin the knight.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays f3 hoping for a big center, but ...dxe4 fxe4 ...e5 challenges it immediately.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...e5, White's center is under pressure. The f-file is open but Black's pieces are active.
lines.hintText: Against 3.f3, take dxe4 fxe4 and then push ...e5 — don't let White build a big center.

lines.title: Two Knights: 3.Nc3 dxe4 4.Nxe4 Nf6
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. Nc3 dxe4 4. Nxe4 Nf6 5. Nxf6+ gxf6 6. Bc4 Bf5 7. Ne2 e6 8. O-O Bd6
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 Nc3 dxe4 Nxe4 Nf6 Nxf6+ gxf6 Bc4 Bf5 Ne2 e6 O-O Bd6
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 d5e4 c3e4 g8f6 e4f6 g7f6 f1c4 c8f5 g1e2 e7e6 e1g1 f8d6
lines.lineSummary: White plays Nxf6 to damage Black's pawn structure. Black recaptures gxf6, keeping the bishop pair and gaining the open g-file for counterplay.
lines.strategicGoal: Recapture gxf6 to keep the bishop pair. Develop ...Bf5, ...e6, ...Bd6 and use the open g-file.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White doubles Black's f-pawns, but Black's bishops and open g-file provide excellent compensation.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black's bishop pair is strong. The open g-file gives Black attacking chances against White's king.
lines.hintText: After Nxf6+, recapture with the g-pawn — the bishop pair and open g-file are your compensation.

lines.title: Trap: Advance ...c5 Break
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. e5 Bf5 4. Nf3 e6 5. Be2 c5 6. O-O Nc6 7. c3 cxd4 8. cxd4 Nge7
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 e5 Bf5 Nf3 e6 Be2 c5 O-O Nc6 c3 cxd4 cxd4 Nge7
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 e4e5 c8f5 g1f3 e7e6 f1e2 c6c5 e1g1 b8c6 c2c3 c5d4 c3d4 g8e7
lines.lineSummary: In the Advance, Black times the ...c5 break to undermine White's center. If White isn't careful, the d4 pawn becomes a target.
lines.strategicGoal: Time the ...c5 break carefully. After cxd4, White's center collapses and Black gets active play.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays c3 to support d4, but after ...cxd4 cxd4, the d4 pawn becomes an isolated weakness.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...cxd4 cxd4, Black targets the isolated d4 pawn. ...Nge7 prepares ...Nf5 hitting d4.
lines.hintText: In the Advance, always prepare ...c5 — it's your main break to challenge White's center.

lines.title: Panov Attack: 3.exd5 cxd5 4.c4
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. exd5 cxd5 4. c4 Nf6 5. Nc3 e6 6. Nf3 Bb4 7. Bd3
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 exd5 cxd5 c4 Nf6 Nc3 e6 Nf3 Bb4 Bd3
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 e4d5 c6d5 c2c4 g8f6 b1c3 e7e6 g1f3 f8b4 f1d3
lines.lineSummary: White plays the Panov Attack with c4, creating an IQP position. Black develops naturally and targets the isolated d4 pawn.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Nf6, ...e6, ...Bb4 to pin the knight. Target the d4 pawn which becomes isolated after cxd5.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White gets active piece play around the IQP, but Black's solid setup neutralizes the initiative.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black targets the d4 pawn. After ...Bb4, the pin on Nc3 creates pressure. White must be precise.
lines.hintText: Against the Panov (4.c4), play ...Nf6, ...e6, ...Bb4 — target the isolated d4 pawn.

lines.title: Classical: ...Nd7 Flexible Setup
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. Nc3 dxe4 4. Nxe4 Nd7 5. Nf3 Ngf6 6. Nxf6+ Nxf6 7. Be2 Bf5
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 Nc3 dxe4 Nxe4 Nd7 Nf3 Ngf6 Nxf6+ Nxf6 Be2 Bf5
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 d5e4 c3e4 b8d7 g1f3 g8f6 e4f6 d7f6 f1e2 c8f5
lines.lineSummary: Instead of 4...Bf5, Black plays 4...Nd7 for a more flexible setup. After ...Ngf6 and the exchange, ...Bf5 develops the bishop actively.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...Nd7 first, then ...Ngf6. After the exchange, develop ...Bf5 with a solid position.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White exchanges knights, but Black's position is very solid with the bishop on f5.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black has a harmonious position with no weaknesses. The light-squared bishop is active on f5.
lines.hintText: 4...Nd7 is a flexible alternative to 4...Bf5 — both are good, this one avoids some sharp lines.

lines.title: Trap: Punishing Scholar's Mate
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. Bc4 d5 3. Bb3 dxe4 4. Qh5 g6 5. Qe2 Nf6
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 Bc4 d5 Bb3 dxe4 Qh5 g6 Qe2 Nf6
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 f1c4 d7d5 c4b3 d5e4 d1h5 g7g6 h5e2 g8f6
lines.lineSummary: White tries a Scholar's Mate-style attack with Bc4-Qh5. Black calmly defends with ...g6 and ...Nf6, and White's queen is misplaced.
lines.strategicGoal: Defend calmly with ...g6 and ...Nf6. White's queen is out too early and Black develops with tempo.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays Bc4 and Qh5 hoping for a quick attack, but Black's Caro-Kann structure is too solid.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...g6 and ...Nf6, White's queen must retreat. Black has gained tempi and has a better position.
lines.hintText: Against early queen attacks, stay calm — ...g6 and ...Nf6 develop while chasing the queen.

lines.title: Short Advance: 3.e5 Bf5 4.Nc3
lines.pgn: 1. e4 c6 2. d4 d5 3. e5 Bf5 4. Nc3 e6 5. g4 Bg6 6. Nge2 c5 7. h4 h5
lines.moveSequenceSan: e4 c6 d4 d5 e5 Bf5 Nc3 e6 g4 Bg6 Nge2 c5 h4 h5
lines.moveSequenceUci: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 e4e5 c8f5 b1c3 e7e6 g2g4 f5g6 g1e2 c6c5 h2h4 h7h5
lines.lineSummary: White plays the aggressive Short Variation with g4 to chase the bishop. Black must know the precise ...c5 and ...h5 counter-breaks.
lines.strategicGoal: After g4, play ...Bg6 and prepare ...c5. The ...h5 counter-thrust is key to stopping White's kingside expansion.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White pushes g4 aggressively, but Black's counterplay with ...c5 and ...h5 is very strong.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black's ...c5 and ...h5 breaks create counterplay on both sides. The position is sharp but Black is fine.
lines.hintText: Against g4, retreat ...Bg6 and play ...c5 and ...h5 — fight back on both flanks.

lines.title: Classical: ...e5 Main Line
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Nf3 O-O 6. Be2 e5 7. O-O Nc6
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 Nf3 O-O Be2 e5 O-O Nc6
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 e7e5 e1g1 b8c6
lines.lineSummary: The main King's Indian position. Black plays ...e5 to challenge the center and develops ...Nc6 to support the e5 push. The position leads to a complex middlegame.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...e5 to challenge the center. After ...Nc6, prepare ...Nd7 and ...f5 for a kingside attack.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White has a space advantage but Black's kingside counterplay with ...f5 is very dangerous.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black prepares ...f5 for a kingside pawn storm. The Bg7 supports the ...e5 pawn and eyes the queenside.
lines.hintText: The key move is ...e5 — this is the heart of the King's Indian. Then prepare ...f5.

lines.title: Saemisch: 5.f3 System
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. f3 O-O 6. Be3 e5 7. d5 Nh5
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 f3 O-O Be3 e5 d5 Nh5
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 f2f3 e8g8 c1e3 e7e5 d4d5 f6h5
lines.lineSummary: White plays the aggressive Saemisch with f3. Black responds with ...e5 and ...Nh5, preparing ...f5 for a kingside attack.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...e5 and ...Nh5 preparing ...f5. The knight on h5 supports the f5 break and can reroute to f4.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White's f3 looks aggressive but weakens the kingside. Black's ...f5 break is very strong.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...Nh5, Black threatens ...f5 with a powerful kingside attack. The knight can go to f4.
lines.hintText: Against the Saemisch (f3), play ...e5 and ...Nh5 — the ...f5 break is your main weapon.

lines.title: Four Pawns Attack: 5.f4
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. f4 O-O 6. Nf3 c5 7. d5 e6
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 f4 O-O Nf3 c5 d5 e6
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 f2f4 e8g8 g1f3 c7c5 d4d5 e7e6
lines.lineSummary: White plays the aggressive Four Pawns Attack. Black strikes with ...c5 and ...e6 to undermine the overextended center.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...c5 and ...e6 to challenge White's overextended center. The pawns look scary but they're vulnerable.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White's four pawns in the center look impressive but are actually overextended and vulnerable to breaks.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...c5 and ...e6, White's center comes under pressure. The d5 pawn can become a target.
lines.hintText: Against the Four Pawns (f4), play ...c5 and ...e6 — attack the overextended center.

lines.title: Fianchetto: 3.Nf3 Bg7 4.g3
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nf3 Bg7 4. g3 O-O 5. Bg2 d6 6. O-O Nbd7 7. Nc3 e5
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nf3 Bg7 g3 O-O Bg2 d6 O-O Nbd7 Nc3 e5
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 g1f3 f8g7 g2g3 e8g8 f1g2 d7d6 e1g1 b8d7 b1c3 e7e5
lines.lineSummary: White plays the Fianchetto System, the most positional approach. Black develops ...Nbd7 and ...e5, maintaining the King's Indian structure.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Nbd7 and ...e5. The fianchetto is less aggressive, so Black has time to set up normally.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays g3 for a quiet game, but Black's ...e5 still creates dynamic counterplay.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black's position is solid with ...e5 controlling the center. The Bg7 is powerful on the long diagonal.
lines.hintText: Against the Fianchetto (g3), develop normally — ...Nbd7, ...e5. The position is less sharp but still rich.

lines.title: vs London: 2.Bf4 System
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. Bf4 g6 3. e3 Bg7 4. Nf3 O-O 5. Be2 d6 6. O-O Nbd7
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 Bf4 g6 e3 Bg7 Nf3 O-O Be2 d6 O-O Nbd7
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c1f4 g7g6 e2e3 f8g7 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 d7d6 e1g1 b8d7
lines.lineSummary: White plays a London System setup. Black develops the King's Indian structure naturally — the London is less challenging than the classical lines.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop normally — ...d6, ...Nbd7, ...e5. The London is less dangerous against the KID than classical setups.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays the London thinking it works against everything, but the KID structure is very comfortable here.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black gets an easy game. After ...e5, the Bf4 must retreat and Black has a good position.
lines.hintText: Against the London, just develop your KID normally — ...d6, ...Nbd7, ...e5. It's comfortable.

lines.title: Mar del Plata: ...f5 Attack
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Nf3 O-O 6. Be2 e5 7. O-O Nc6 8. d5 Ne7 9. Ne1 Nd7 10. f3 f5
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 Nf3 O-O Be2 e5 O-O Nc6 d5 Ne7 Ne1 Nd7 f3 f5
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 e7e5 e1g1 b8c6 d4d5 c6e7 f3e1 f6d7 f2f3 f7f5
lines.lineSummary: The famous Mar del Plata Attack — Black's kingside pawn storm with ...f5. This is the most theoretically important King's Indian line.
lines.strategicGoal: After d5 closes the center, play ...Ne7 and ...f5 for a kingside attack. The center is locked, so flank play decides.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays d5 to close the center and prepares queenside expansion. Black counters with ...f5 on the kingside.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black's ...f5 opens the f-file for the rook and creates a kingside attack. The race between flanks is the key theme.
lines.hintText: After d5 closes the center, reroute with ...Ne7 and push ...f5 — the kingside attack is your plan.

lines.title: Trap: Premature dxe5
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Nf3 O-O 6. Be2 e5 7. dxe5 dxe5 8. Qxd8 Rxd8 9. Nxe5 Nxe4
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 Nf3 O-O Be2 e5 dxe5 dxe5 Qxd8 Rxd8 Nxe5 Nxe4
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 e7e5 d4e5 d6e5 d1d8 f8d8 f3e5 f6e4
lines.lineSummary: White captures dxe5 prematurely and grabs the e5 pawn. But Black wins it back with ...Nxe4, exploiting the pin on the Nc3.
lines.strategicGoal: If White takes dxe5 dxe5 Qxd8 Rxd8 Nxe5, play ...Nxe4 — the Nc3 is pinned by the Bg7.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White grabs the e5 pawn thinking it's free, but the Bg7 pins the Nc3 and ...Nxe4 wins it back with interest.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...Nxe4, the Nc3 is pinned. Black wins back the pawn with a better position.
lines.hintText: If White takes dxe5 and then Nxe5, check if ...Nxe4 works — the Bg7 pin is powerful.

lines.title: Averbakh: 5.Be2 O-O 6.Bg5
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Be2 O-O 6. Bg5 c5 7. d5 h6 8. Bf4 e6
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 Be2 O-O Bg5 c5 d5 h6 Bf4 e6
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 f1e2 e8g8 c1g5 c7c5 d4d5 h7h6 g5f4 e7e6
lines.lineSummary: White plays the Averbakh with Bg5, pinning the knight. Black plays ...c5 and ...h6 to challenge the bishop, then ...e6 to break the center.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...c5 and ...h6 to challenge the Bg5. Then ...e6 to break open the center.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White pins the knight with Bg5, but Black's ...h6 and ...c5 create counterplay.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...h6 Bf4 ...e6, Black gets active play in the center. The position is dynamically balanced.
lines.hintText: Against the Averbakh (Bg5), play ...c5 and ...h6 — challenge the bishop and break with ...e6.

lines.title: Bayonet Attack: 9.b4
lines.pgn: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Nf3 O-O 6. Be2 e5 7. O-O Nc6 8. d5 Ne7 9. b4 Nh5 10. g3 f5
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 Nf6 c4 g6 Nc3 Bg7 e4 d6 Nf3 O-O Be2 e5 O-O Nc6 d5 Ne7 b4 Nh5 g3 f5
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 e7e5 e1g1 b8c6 d4d5 c6e7 b2b4 f6h5 g2g3 f7f5
lines.lineSummary: White plays the Bayonet Attack with b4, the most aggressive anti-KID weapon. Black responds with ...Nh5 and ...f5 for the kingside counter.
lines.strategicGoal: After b4, play ...Nh5 and ...f5 immediately — don't let White's queenside attack develop without a fight.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White pushes b4 for a queenside attack, but Black's kingside counter with ...f5 is equally dangerous.
lines.punishmentIdea: The position is a race — White attacks on the queenside, Black on the kingside. Timing is everything.
lines.hintText: Against the Bayonet (b4), play ...Nh5 and ...f5 immediately — speed is critical.

lines.title: Main Idea: ...c5 Challenge
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 Nc6 4. c3 Nf6 5. Nd2 e6 6. Ngf3 Bd6
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 Nc6 c3 Nf6 Nd2 e6 Ngf3 Bd6
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 b8c6 c2c3 g8f6 b1d2 e7e6 g1f3 f8d6
lines.lineSummary: The core Anti-London idea: play ...c5 immediately to challenge d4. White's Bf4 is less effective when the center is under pressure.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...c5 to challenge d4 immediately. Develop ...Nc6, ...Nf6, ...Bd6 to exchange the dark-squared bishop.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays the London but ...c5 challenges the center before White can consolidate.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...Bd6, Black offers to exchange the dark-squared bishops. This neutralizes White's main London asset.
lines.hintText: Against the London, play ...c5 early — challenge the center before White settles in.

lines.title: Bishop Exchange: ...Bd6 Plan
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 Nc6 4. Nf3 Nf6 5. c3 e6 6. Bd3 Bd6 7. Bxd6 Qxd6 8. O-O O-O
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 Nc6 Nf3 Nf6 c3 e6 Bd3 Bd6 Bxd6 Qxd6 O-O O-O
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 b8c6 g1f3 g8f6 c2c3 e7e6 f1d3 f8d6 f4d6 d8d6 e1g1 e8g8
lines.lineSummary: Black develops ...Bd6 to force the exchange of dark-squared bishops. After Bxd6 Qxd6, Black has a comfortable position with easy development.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Bd6 to exchange White's London bishop. After the exchange, Black's position is very comfortable.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White's London bishop gets exchanged, removing the main strategic asset of the London System.
lines.punishmentIdea: After the bishop exchange, Black has a balanced position with no weaknesses. Development is easy.
lines.hintText: The key idea: develop ...Bd6 to exchange White's bishop — this is the Anti-London's main weapon.

lines.title: ...Qb6 Pressure Line
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 Nc6 4. Nf3 Qb6 5. Nc3 cxd4 6. exd4 Nf6 7. Be2 Bf5
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 Nc6 Nf3 Qb6 Nc3 cxd4 exd4 Nf6 Be2 Bf5
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 b8c6 g1f3 d8b6 b1c3 c5d4 e3d4 g8f6 f1e2 c8f5
lines.lineSummary: Black plays ...Qb6 to pressure b2 and d4 simultaneously. White must be careful about the b2 pawn.
lines.strategicGoal: Play ...Qb6 to pressure b2 and d4. White must defend accurately or lose a pawn.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White often forgets that b2 is weak in the London. ...Qb6 exploits this immediately.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...Qb6, White's b2 pawn is under pressure. If White plays Nc3, ...cxd4 exd4 leaves d4 weak too.
lines.hintText: ...Qb6 is a strong move — it attacks b2 and puts pressure on d4 at the same time.

lines.title: Trap: b2 Pawn Grab
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 Qb6 4. Nc3 Qxb2 5. Nb5 Na6 6. Rb1 Qa3
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 Qb6 Nc3 Qxb2 Nb5 Na6 Rb1 Qa3
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 d8b6 b1c3 b6b2 c3b5 b8a6 a1b1 b2a3
lines.lineSummary: Black grabs the b2 pawn with ...Qxb2. White gets compensation with Nb5 threatening Nc7+, but Black can hold with precise play.
lines.strategicGoal: Grab the b2 pawn if White allows it — but be ready for Nb5 threats. ...Na6 covers c7.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays Nc3 without protecting b2. Black can grab it, but must know the follow-up.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...Qxb2 Nb5, play ...Na6 to cover c7. The queen escapes to a3 and Black is up a pawn.
lines.hintText: If White plays Nc3 without protecting b2, grab it — but know that Nb5 is coming.

lines.title: vs d5 Close: Solid Approach
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 Nc6 4. c3 e6 5. Nd2 Nf6 6. Ngf3 Be7 7. Bd3 O-O
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 Nc6 c3 e6 Nd2 Nf6 Ngf3 Be7 Bd3 O-O
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 b8c6 c2c3 e7e6 b1d2 g8f6 g1f3 f8e7 f1d3 e8g8
lines.lineSummary: White plays a solid setup with c3-Nd2-Ngf3. Black develops naturally and reaches a comfortable middlegame.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...e6, ...Nf6, ...Be7, and castle. The position is balanced and Black has easy play.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays solidly but Black's position is very comfortable with natural development.
lines.punishmentIdea: Black has no weaknesses and easy development. The position is balanced with chances for both sides.
lines.hintText: Against the solid c3-Nd2 setup, just develop naturally — ...e6, ...Nf6, ...Be7, castle.

lines.title: ...Bf5 Mirror: Fight for Light Squares
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 Bf5 3. e3 e6 4. Nf3 Nf6 5. Bd3 Bxd3 6. Qxd3 Bd6 7. Bxd6 Qxd6
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 Bf5 e3 e6 Nf3 Nf6 Bd3 Bxd3 Qxd3 Bd6 Bxd6 Qxd6
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c8f5 e2e3 e7e6 g1f3 g8f6 f1d3 f5d3 d1d3 f8d6 f4d6 d8d6
lines.lineSummary: Black mirrors White's bishop development with ...Bf5. After the bishop exchanges, the position is completely equal.
lines.strategicGoal: Develop ...Bf5 to mirror White's setup. Exchange bishops and reach a balanced position.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White plays the London but Black mirrors with ...Bf5, leading to mass exchanges and equality.
lines.punishmentIdea: After both bishop pairs are exchanged, the position is dead equal. Black has no problems.
lines.hintText: ...Bf5 is the simplest Anti-London — mirror White's bishop and exchange everything.

lines.title: Open Center: ...cxd4 Lines
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 cxd4 4. exd4 Nc6 5. Nf3 Nf6 6. c3 Bf5 7. Bd3 Bxd3 8. Qxd3 e6
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 cxd4 exd4 Nc6 Nf3 Nf6 c3 Bf5 Bd3 Bxd3 Qxd3 e6
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 c5d4 e3d4 b8c6 g1f3 g8f6 c2c3 c8f5 f1d3 f5d3 d1d3 e7e6
lines.lineSummary: Black captures ...cxd4 to open the center. After exd4, the position resembles a Queen's Gambit Exchange with the bishop already committed to f4.
lines.strategicGoal: Capture ...cxd4 to open the center. Develop ...Bf5 to exchange the light-squared bishops.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White recaptures exd4 but the bishop on f4 is less effective in the open center.
lines.punishmentIdea: After the exchanges, Black has a comfortable position. The open center suits Black's active pieces.
lines.hintText: ...cxd4 opens the center — good when you want an active game instead of a slow buildup.

lines.title: Trap: ...h5 Bishop Chase
lines.pgn: 1. d4 d5 2. Bf4 c5 3. e3 Nc6 4. Nf3 e6 5. Bd3 Bd6 6. Bg3 Nf6 7. O-O h5
lines.moveSequenceSan: d4 d5 Bf4 c5 e3 Nc6 Nf3 e6 Bd3 Bd6 Bg3 Nf6 O-O h5
lines.moveSequenceUci: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 c7c5 e2e3 b8c6 g1f3 e7e6 f1d3 f8d6 f4g3 g8f6 e1g1 h7h5
lines.lineSummary: After White retreats Bg3, Black plays the aggressive ...h5 threatening ...h4 to trap the bishop. White must be very careful.
lines.strategicGoal: After Bg3, play ...h5 threatening ...h4 to trap the bishop. White must give up the bishop pair or weaken the kingside.
lines.commonOpponentMistake: White retreats Bg3 thinking it's safe, but ...h5-h4 threatens to trap the bishop.
lines.punishmentIdea: After ...h5, White must deal with ...h4. If Bxd6 Qxd6, Black has the bishop pair. If the bishop stays, ...h4 traps it.
lines.hintText: After Bg3, play ...h5 — the threat of ...h4 trapping the bishop is very strong.
